meta.description: Quantify immune cell infiltration per sample using single-sample GSEA (ssGSEA) with curated immune cell gene signatures. Inputs: expression matrix. Outputs: immune cell score matrix, heatmap, violin comparison plots, correlation with clinical variables.

scientific_integrity.detail: Scientific integrity check completed without reportable redline findings.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill explicitly excludes clinical decision making and produced only descriptive analytic outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow correctly frames ssGSEA/GSVA as relative enrichment and correlation as hypothesis-generating rather than causal inference.

code_usability.detail: The R pipeline executed successfully across packaged tests, five audit inputs, and the output-directory reuse regression scenario.

functional_suitability.note: Functional suitability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

reliability.note: The stale-plot rerun bug is fixed; only a minor no-plot summary wording ambiguity remains in run_record.txt.

performance_context.note: Canonical plotted runs remain moderately heavy but acceptable for the packaged example data.

agent_usability.note: Agent usability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

human_usability.note: The skill is easy to follow; the empty plot directory behavior could be documented more explicitly.

security.note: Security was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

maintainability.note: Regression coverage now protects both malformed inputs and output-directory reuse semantics.

agent_specific.note: The skill is now effectively idempotent for reruns; only the no-plot summary wording remains slightly imprecise.

key_strengths: The previous output-directory reuse bug is fixed and now protected by an explicit regression test.
Generated manifests are now aligned with the documented output inventory, including run_record.txt and output_manifest.txt.
Repeated canonical runs with the same seed produced identical hashes for key result tables, supporting deterministic behavior.

recommendations.title: Clarify empty plot directory behavior in no-plot runs
recommendations.problem: When --make_plots=false, outputs are correct and the manifest is clean, but run_record.txt still says outputs are under data/, table/, and plot/. Because plot/ is created eagerly, users may assume figures should exist there.
recommendations.root_cause: The output summary string is fixed and the plot directory is created before the plotting branch is evaluated.
recommendations.fix: Either create plot/ lazily only when plots are requested, or document explicitly that plot/ may exist but remain empty when --make_plots=false.
